title: [FIRRTL] Firtool may crash when compiling a certain fir file.
body:
## Environment
OS: Ubuntu 22.04
firtool: 1.62.0 ~ 1.75.0
mill: 0.11.2
chisel: 6.5.0
firrtl: 3.3.0

## Repeat the crash
```bash
git clone https://github.com/Siudya/riscv-boom
cd riscv-boom
make chirrtl # success
make firrtl # success
make hw # failed
make verilog # failed
make systemverilog # failed
make rtl # failed
```

The failure log is as following:
```
ExitCode:
139
STDOUT:

STDERR:
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/circt and include the crash backtrace.
Stack dump without symbol names (ensure you have llvm-symbolizer in your PATH or set the environment var `LLVM_SYMBOLIZER_PATH` to point to it):
0  firtool   0x00000000006ffed7 llvm::sys::PrintStackTrace(llvm::raw_ostream&, int) + 39
1  firtool   0x00000000006fdc8e llvm::sys::RunSignalHandlers() + 238
2  firtool   0x000000000070080f
3  libc.so.6 0x0000753698a42520
4  firtool   0x0000000000b253dd
5  firtool   0x0000000000b1d6af
6  firtool   0x0000000000b1837a
7  firtool   0x0000000000b3ce19
8  firtool   0x00000000007673dc
9  firtool   0x000000000113dcfc llvm::ThreadPool::processTasks(llvm::ThreadPoolTaskGroup*) + 828
10 firtool   0x000000000113e85c
11 libc.so.6 0x0000753698a94ac3
12 libc.so.6 0x0000753698b26850
```

## Other results
I have tried manually call firtool to compile imtermediate files and I got these:
```bash
firtool -o RawTileTop.fir.mlir --format fir --disable-annotation-unknown --ir-fir build/chirrtl/RawTileTop.fir # success
firtool -o RawTileTop.hw.mlir --format fir --disable-annotation-unknown --ir-hw build/chirrtl/RawTileTop.fir # failed
firtool -o RawTileTop.sv --format fir --disable-annotation-unknown --verilog build/chirrtl/RawTileTop.fir # failed

firtool -o RawTileTop.hw.mlir --format mlir --ir-hw build/firrtl/RawTileTop.fir.mlir # success
firtool -o RawTileTop.sv --format mlir --verilog build/firrtl/RawTileTop.fir.mlir # success
```
It seems firtool will crash somewhere when translate fir to hw-ir.
